description: As Army Artillery with the 1st Panzer Army, crosses the Dutch border at Elten together with the SS — beginning of the Western Campaign.
First time taking up a firing position, bombardment of Fort Westerfort. After crossing the IJssel, continues on at great speed.
Advance comes to a halt at the Grebbe Line; firing almost 3 days without pause — 1000 tons of shells and 30 Stukas break the position.
On the last day before the capitulation, the entire 11th Battery forms a shock troop up to 10 km from Utrecht, facing a Dutch brigade which surrenders.
3 days' rest after the Holland fighting.
One day in Turnhout.
In a beautiful North Sea resort; the 11th Battery sinks a picket boat. Limber position; haven't seen the sea yet.
3 days near Antwerp; after 5 hours of sleep straight into the firing position.
Firing position, beginning of the Battle of Flanders. Advanced about 8 km daily; shelling of Thielt, Gynze, Diksmuide and Dunkirk.
Days of major fighting before Dunkirk, the English are withdrawing. Observation and shelling, fabulous air battles with Messerschmitts. Took part in the Lys crossing.
After driving via Ypres, Lille, Douai to Arras for rest near Essen. Recover with food, sleep, football, Skat and music.
Resting. Everyone slaughters and roasts chickens, pigeons and hares; the field kitchen takes a calf and a pig. Then march via Roye, Montdidier, Beauvais toward Paris.
The IV./AR 256 is among the first troops to march into Paris, a grand parade on the Place de la Concorde.
Pushes with a division's vanguard almost to Orléans, briefly deployed as an infantry shock troop, takes 300 prisoners. Lt. Kästner killed in action.
After day-marches of 160-200 km via Versailles and Chartres to Angers. Fired for the last time there; Wilhelm has a fever and diarrhea.
Were supposed to go to Biarritz, then re-routed. Waiting time in dumb cow-towns. Wilhelm is promoted to Gefreiter.
Transferred to nice quarters, town about the size of Glauchau. Strolls through town, cinema, lunch — finally something civilized. In the cafés, pretty, heavily made-up girls.

place: Sillé-le-Guillaume ('Wilhelmsruhe', 40 km N of Le Mans)
description: Via Thouars, Saumur, La Flèche, Le Mans, transferred to an old castle, later moves into a barracks camp (forest camp) with bathing facilities.
Wilhelm is on his first leave since the beginning of the war, with Marianne, and meets his daughter Helga for the first time.
After leave, 24 hours in a 2nd-class compartment via Luxembourg to Argentan, then to quarters. Spa town Bagnoles nearby. End of October, main dressing station with guard duty.
About 200 km west of Tessé for live-fire exercises at the training grounds. Wilhelm as ammunition driver without much to do.

Early in the morning, 200 km north; firing position as coastal defense. On the heights above the harbor, where German fighters are very busy at the airfield. From 1940-12-06 on, working as clerk at the motor-transport workshop (Schirrmeisterei). Christmas with roast pork, Stollen, and sparkling wine.
